description: Scaffold the project and implement the backend: Express server, weather service that calls Open-Meteo (geocoding + forecast) with a mock-data fallback, JSON API endpoints, and vitest unit tests with mocked fetch. After this feature, `npm install`, `npm test`, and `MOCK_MODE=true npm start` all work; the server returns valid JSON for /api/weather even with no network.
steps: Create package.json at repo root with: "type": "module", "engines": { "node": ">=20" }, scripts { "start": "node server/index.js", "test": "vitest run", "test:watch": "vitest" }, dependency express ^4.19.2, devDependency vitest ^2.1.0. Set name to "weather-app" and version "0.1.0". Run `npm install` to generate package-lock.json and node_modules.

Create .env.example documenting two variables: PORT (default 3000) and MOCK_MODE (set to 'true' to force using bundled mock data instead of calling Open-Meteo). Include a comment explaining MOCK_MODE is also auto-enabled when the upstream fetch fails.

Create server/mockData.js exporting two functions: mockGeocode(city) returns { name, country, latitude, longitude, timezone } for a fixed sample city (e.g., London at 51.5074, -0.1278, Europe/London) regardless of input, and mockForecast(lat, lon) returns a payload with current { temperature_2m, weather_code, wind_speed_10m, time } and daily { time: [7 ISO dates], temperature_2m_max: [7 numbers], temperature_2m_min: [7 numbers], weather_code: [7 codes], precipitation_sum: [7 numbers] }. Use plausible numbers. Also export weatherCodeToText(code) mapping the WMO codes used in the mock (0 Clear, 1-3 Partly cloudy, 45/48 Fog, 51/53/55 Drizzle, 61/63/65 Rain, 71/73/75 Snow, 80/81/82 Rain showers, 95 Thunderstorm) to short human-readable strings; default to 'Unknown'.

Create server/weather.js exporting async function getWeather(city, { mockMode = false, fetchImpl = globalThis.fetch } = {}). Logic: if mockMode is true, build the response from mockGeocode + mockForecast and return immediately. Otherwise call https://geocoding-api.open-meteo.com/v1/search?name=<encoded>&count=1&language=en&format=json using fetchImpl; if results array is empty throw new Error('City not found') with a .code = 'NOT_FOUND' property; then call https://api.open-meteo.com/v1/forecast with latitude, longitude, current=temperature_2m,weather_code,wind_speed_10m, daily=temperature_2m_max,temperature_2m_min,weather_code,precipitation_sum, timezone=auto, forecast_days=7. Wrap the two network calls in a try/catch: on any network failure (or non-2xx response) other than NOT_FOUND, fall back to building the response from mockData and set source: 'mock-fallback' on the returned object. The normal shape is: { source: 'open-meteo' | 'mock' | 'mock-fallback', location: { name, country, latitude, longitude, timezone }, current: { temperature, windSpeed, weatherCode, condition, time }, daily: [ { date, tempMax, tempMin, weatherCode, condition, precipitation } x7 ] }. Use weatherCodeToText to populate condition strings. NOT_FOUND must propagate (do not fall back to mock for that case).

Create server/routes.js exporting a function registerRoutes(app) that adds: GET /api/health -> { status: 'ok' }; GET /api/weather?city=<name> -> calls getWeather; on success returns 200 JSON; on Error with code 'NOT_FOUND' returns 404 { error: 'City not found' }; on missing/empty city query returns 400 { error: 'Query parameter "city" is required' }; on any other error returns 500 { error: 'Internal error' } and console.error the actual error. Read MOCK_MODE from process.env each request (process.env.MOCK_MODE === 'true').

Create server/index.js as the entry point: import express, create app, app.use(express.json()), app.use(express.static('public')) where 'public' is resolved relative to the file using import.meta.url + path.dirname/path.join so it works regardless of cwd, call registerRoutes(app), then app.listen(process.env.PORT || 3000, () => console.log(`Weather app listening on http://localhost:${port}`)). Export app for testing if convenient but the listen call must run when invoked as the entry script.

Create tests/weather.test.js using vitest. Tests: (1) returns mock data when mockMode: true is passed, with source 'mock' and 7 daily entries; (2) calls geocoding then forecast URLs in order when given a fake fetchImpl that returns successful Response-like objects, and produces source 'open-meteo' with mapped condition strings; (3) throws an error with code 'NOT_FOUND' when geocoding returns an empty results array (no fallback to mock); (4) returns source 'mock-fallback' when fetchImpl rejects on the geocoding call; (5) weatherCodeToText returns 'Clear' for 0 and 'Unknown' for an unknown code like 999. Build fetchImpl as a vi.fn() that returns objects shaped like { ok: true, status: 200, json: async () => (...) } - do NOT use real network.

Create README.md with sections: Overview (one paragraph), Quick start (`npm install`, `npm start`, open http://localhost:3000), Mock mode (`MOCK_MODE=true npm start` to run without internet, also auto-engages on upstream errors), API (document GET /api/health and GET /api/weather?city=...), Tests (`npm test`), and a Notes section listing tech choices (Node 20+, Express, Open-Meteo, vitest).
acceptance_criteria: `npm install` completes without errors and produces node_modules/ and package-lock.json.
`npm test` runs vitest and all tests in tests/weather.test.js pass.

`MOCK_MODE=true PORT=3000 node server/index.js` starts the server; `curl -s 'http://localhost:3000/api/weather?city=anything'` returns 200 JSON with fields location.name, current.temperature, daily (length 7), and source equal to 'mock'.
`curl -s 'http://localhost:3000/api/weather'` (no city param) returns HTTP 400 with body { "error": "Query parameter \"city\" is required" }.
weather.js never throws on upstream network failures except for the NOT_FOUND case; instead it returns a response with source 'mock-fallback'.
verification: Run `npm install` from the repo root and confirm exit code 0.
Run `npm test` and confirm all vitest tests pass.
findings: Implemented backend per spec. npm install succeeded (114 packages, 5 moderate audit warnings from transitive deps - not addressed). vitest run passed all 6 tests. Server smoke-tested with MOCK_MODE=true PORT=3000: GET /api/weather?city=London returned 200 with source 'mock' and 7 daily entries, GET /api/weather returned 400 with the required error body, GET /api/health returned {"status":"ok"}. server/index.js uses an isEntry check (resolving process.argv[1]) so the app can also be imported by tests if needed without auto-listening. node_modules/ and .env are gitignored; package-lock.json is committed.
